category_title: Academically at-risk primary students disengaged from traditional schooling
minimal_context: The student is participating in an after-school session within a 3D virtual environment and is expected to complete an academic quest by submitting a digital document with reflection notes.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open your digital document and start writing your reflection notes for the assigned quest.

STUDENT: I want to get what my classmate has. The bird on the shoulder… That one needs how many lumins?

TUTOR: Let's focus on the assignment right now.

STUDENT: ... the 3D world is very fun. Can I just have a little more time to explore before I do the spreadsheet?

TUTOR: You need to complete the academic work first.

STUDENT: I like the 3D space as I can play with airplane, car, and boat. (Moves avatar away from the quest area to play with the virtual vehicles instead of opening the document)

options.A: Give the student a menu of different quest tasks (e.g., writing reflection notes, completing the spreadsheet) to choose from, providing the option to change tasks at any time during the session.
options.B: Send the student to a quiet virtual space to fill out a 'Cool Down Document' describing the infraction and what to do differently, reviewing it during the next unstructured period.
options.C: Constantly remind the student, with determination, to refocus on their academic quest and digital document during the session.
options.D: Ignore the student's off-task behavior (where safe) and reinforce peers who are actively working on their digital documents during the session.
